name: Product Card Style
description: Visual design of product cards: corners, shadows, image ratio, text weight, button shape
current_approach: Dark card (#1c1717) with subtle border, 14px radius, 160px image height, bold name (weight 600), full-red Add to Cart button, GSAP hover lift y:-4
hypothesis: Card elevation, image aspect ratio, or typography contrast could be improved to make cards feel more premium and more obviously shoppable

name: Chat Bubble Style
description: Visual treatment of user and agent message bubbles: colors, radius, tails, padding
current_approach: User: solid red (#e63329) bg, white text, 18px radius with flat bottom-right corner. Agent: dark card (#1c1717) bg, white text, 18px radius with flat bottom-left corner. Padding 14px 18px.
hypothesis: Bubble contrast, border treatment, or tail shape could make the conversation feel more personal and less clinical

name: Typography & Spacing
description: Font sizing, weights, line heights, and spacing between chat elements
current_approach: Inter 15px body, Playfair Display for brand name, gap 20px between messages, bubbles at 14.5px Inter, agent avatar 30px circle
hypothesis: Tighter tracking, adjusted font size hierarchy, or different gap values could create a more premium reading experience

name: Color Accent Usage
description: How and where the Kapruka red (#e63329) is applied across the interface
current_approach: Red on: brand name, user bubbles, prices, send button, mic button, Add to Cart button, agent avatar, cart badge, typing dots, pulsing brand dot
hypothesis: Red may be slightly overused — strategic reduction to 3-4 key elements could make the accent feel more deliberate and premium

name: Animation Timing & Feel
description: GSAP animation durations, easing, and stagger values across all animated elements
current_approach: Message: y:12 0.3s power2.out. Card stagger: y:16 stagger:0.07 duration:0.4 power2.out. Card hover: y:-4 0.2s. Cart open: 0.35s power3.out. Cart close: 0.3s power3.in.
hypothesis: Micro-adjustments to easing curves or stagger values could make the UI feel more fluid or snappier depending on direction

name: Input Bar Design
description: Visual design of the message input area: shape, background, button sizing, padding, focus state
current_approach: Dark (#231c1c) input container with 14px radius, red send button (38px circle), red mic button outline (38px circle), 1px border, red accent on focus
hypothesis: The input bar background or button visual weight could be adjusted to feel more refined and less boxy

name: Onboarding Screen
description: The first screen users see: brand lockup, conversation bubbles, input field, background
current_approach: Centered max-width 480px, Kapruka brand name in red Playfair Display 26px, animated red dot, conversation bubbles, input row at bottom, animated canvas background
hypothesis: The brand lockup size, vertical spacing, or bubble visual treatment could create a stronger first impression

name: Mobile Layout
description: How the UI adapts at 375px width: padding, card sizes, bubble max-widths, font adjustments
current_approach: Product cards 175px wide, images 140px tall, bubbles max-width 88%, font 14px for bubbles, padding 16px 14px with 140px bottom clearance for input
hypothesis: Card widths, padding balance, or touch target sizes at mobile viewport could be optimised for better usability and visual density
